options.D: Both Statement-1 and Statement-2 are not sufficient to answer the Question

questions.question: Consider the Question and two Statements given below: Question: What is the age of Manisha? Statement-1: Manisha is 24 years younger than her mother. Statement-2: 5 years later, the ages of Manisha and her mother will be in the ratio 3:5. Which one of the following is correct in respect off the Question and the Statements?

options.A: Statement-1 alone is sufficient to answer the Question
options.B: Statement-2 alone is sufficient to answer the Question
options.C: Both Statement-1 and Statement-2 are sufficient to answer the Question
options.D: Both Statement-1 and Statement-2 are not sufficient to answer the Question

questions.question: Six lectures A, B. C, D, E and F, each of one hour duration, are scheduled between 8:00 a.m. and 2:00 p.m. Consider the Question and two Statements given below: Question: Which lecture is in the third period? Statement-1: Lecture F is preceded by A and followed by C. Statement-2: There is no lecture after lecture B. Which one of the following is correct in respect off the Question and the Statements?

options.A: Statement-1 alone is sufficient to answer the Question
options.B: Statement-2 alone is sufficient to answer the Question
options.C: Both Statement-1 and Statement-2 are sufficient to answer the Question
options.D: Both Statement-1 and Statement-2 are not sufficient to answer the Question

questions.question: A Statement followed by Conclusion-I and Conclusion-II is given below. You have to take the Statement to be true even if it seems to be at variance from the commonly known facts. Read all Conclusions and then decide which of the given Conclusion(s) logically follows/follow from the Statement, disregarding the commonly known facts. Statement: Some radios are mobiles. All mobiles are computers. Some computers are watches. Conclusion-I: Certainly, some radios are watches. Conclusion-II: Certainly, some mobiles are watches. Which one of the following is correct?

A statement followed by Conclusion-I and Conclusion-II is given below. You have to take the statement to be true even if it seems to be at variance from the commonly known facts. Read all conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusion(s) logically follows from the statement, disregarding the commonly known facts. Statement: Some cats are almirahs. Some almirahs are chairs. All chairs are tables. Conclusion-I: Certainly, some almirahs are tables. Conclusion-II: Some cats may not be chairs. Which one of the following is correct?

Consider two Statements and a Question: Statement-1: Priya is 4 ranks below Seema and is 31st from the bottom. Statement-2: Ena is 2 ranks above Seema and is 37th from the bottom. Question: What is Seema's rank from the top in the class of 40 students? Which one of the following is correct in respect off the Statements and the Question?

options.A: Statement-1 alone is not sufficient answer the Question
options.B: Statement-2 alone is not sufficient to answers the Question
options.C: Either Statement-1 alone or Statement-2 alone is sufficient to answers the Question
options.D: Both Statement-1 and Statement-2 are required to answer the Question

questions.question: Consider two Statements and a Question: Statement-1: Each of A and D is heavier than each of B, E and F, but none of them is the heaviest. Statement-2: A is heavier than D, but is lighter than C. Question: Who is the heaviest among A, B, C, D and E? Which one of the following is correct in respect off the Statements and the Question?

options.A: Statement-1 alone is sufficient to answer the Question
options.B: Statement-2 alone is sufficient to answer the Question
options.C: Both Statement-1 and Statement-2 are required to answer the Question
options.D: Neither Statement-1 alone nor Statement-2 alone is sufficient to answer the Question

questions.question: Consider two Statements and a Question: Statement-1: The last day of the month is a Wednesday. Statement-2: The third Saturday of the month was the seventeenth day. Question: What day is the fourteenth of the given month? Which one of the following is correct in respect off the Statements and the Question?

options.A: Statement-1 alone is sufficient to answer the Question
options.B: Statement-2 alone is sufficient to answer the Question
options.C: Both Statement-1 and Statement-2 are required to answer the Question
options.D: Neither Statement-1 alone nor Statement-2 alone is sufficient to answer the Question

questions.question: Two statements S1 and S2 are given below followed by a Question: S1: There are not more than two figures on any page of a 51-page book S2: There is at least one figure on every page. Question: Are there more than 100 figures in that book? Which one of the following is correct in respect off the above Statements and the Question?

options.A: Both S1 and S2 are sufficient to answer the Question, but neither S1 alone nor S2 alone is sufficient to answer the Question.
options.B: S1 alone is sufficient to answer the Question.
options.C: S1 and S2 together are not sufficient to answer the Question.
options.D: S2 alone is sufficient to answer the Question.

questions.question: Two Statements S1 and S2 are given below with regard to four numbers P, Q, R and S followed by a Question: S1: R is greater-than P as well as Q. S2: S is not the largest one. Among four numbers P, Q, R and S which one is the largest? Which one of the following is correct in respect off the above Statements and the Question?

options.A: S1 alone is sufficient to answer the Question.
options.B: S2 alone is sufficient to answer the Question.
options.C: S1 and S2 together are sufficient to answer the Question, but neither S1 alone nor S2 alone is sufficient to answer the Question.
options.D: S1 and S2 together are not sufficient to answer the Question.

questions.question: Two Statements S1 and S2 are given below followed by a Question: S1: n is a prime number. S2: n leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 4. If n is a unique natural number between 10 and 20, then what is n? Which one of the following is correct in respect off the above Statements and the Question?

options.A: S1 alone is sufficient to answer the Question.
options.B: S2 alone is sufficient to answer the Question.
options.C: S1 and S2 together are sufficient to answer the Question, but neither S1 alone nor S2 alone is sufficient to answer the Question.
options.D: S1 and S2 together are not sufficient to answer the Question.

questions.question: Two Statements S1 and S2 are given below with regard to two numbers followed by a Question: S1: Their product is 21. S2: Their sum 10. Question: What are the two numbers? Which one of the following is correct in respect off the above Statements and the Question?

options.A: S1 alone is sufficient to answer the Question.
options.B: S2 alone is sufficient to answer the Question.
options.C: S1 and S2 together are sufficient to answer the Question, but neither S1 alone nor S2 alone is sufficient to answer the Question.
options.D: S1 and S2 together are not sufficient to answer the Question.

questions.question: Three Statements S1, S2 and S3 are given below followed by a Question: S1: C is younger than D, but older than A and B. S2: D is the oldest. S3: A is older than B. Question: Who among A, B, C and D is the youngest? Which one of the following is correct in respect off the above Statements and the Question?

options.A: S1 alone is sufficient to answer the Question.
options.B: S1 and S2 together are sufficient to answer the Question.
options.C: S2 and S3 together are sufficient to answer the Question.
options.D: S1 and S3 together are sufficient to answer the Question.

questions.question: A five-storeyed building with floors from I to V is painted using four different colours and only one colour is used to paint a floor. Consider the following statements: 1. The middle three floors are painted in different colours. 2. The second (II) and the fourth (IV) floors are painted in different colours. 3. The first (I) and the fifth (V) floors are painted red. To ensure that any two consecutive floors have different colours

options.C: Statement 1 is not sufficient, but statement 1 along with statement 2 is sufficient
options.D: Statement 3 is not sufficient, but statement 3 along with statement 2 is sufficient.

questions.question: Consider two statements S1 and S2 followed by a question: S1: p and q both are prime numbers. S2: p + q is an odd integer. Which one of the following is correct?

options.A: S1 alone is sufficient to answer the question
options.B: S2 alone is sufficient to answer the question
options.C: Both S1 and S2 taken together are not sufficient to answer the question
options.D: Both S1 and S2 are necessary to answer the question

questions.question: Consider the following: Statement: Good voice is a natural gift but one has to keep practising to improve and excel well in the field of music. Question: Is pq an odd integer? Conclusions: I. Natural gifts need nurturing and care. II. Even though one's voice is not good; one can keep practising. Which one of the following is correct, in respect of the above statement and conclusions?

options.A: Only conclusion I follows from the statement.
options.B: Only conclusion II follows from the statement.
options.C: Either conclusion I or conclusion II follows from the statement.
options.D: Neither conclusion I nor conclusion II follows from the statement.

questions.question: "Rights are certain advantageous conditions of social well-being indispensable to the true development of the citizen." In the light of this statement, which one of the following is the correct understanding of rights?

options.A: Rights aim at individual good only.
options.B: Rights aim at social good only.
options.C: Rights aim at both individual and social good.
options.D: Rights aim at individual good devoid of social well-being.

questions.question: A ate grapes and pineapple; B ate grapes and oranges; C ate oranges, pineapple, and apple; D ate grapes, apple, and pineapple. After taking fruits, B and C fell sick. In the light of the above facts, it can be said that the cause of sickness was:

Consider the following statements. 1. The rate of population growth is increasing in the country. 2. The death rate is declining faster in the country compared to birth rate. 3. The birth rate is declining faster in the country compared to death rate. 4. Rural-urban migration is taking place regularly in the country. Which one of the following conclusions may be true in the light of the above facts?

options.A: The rate of population growth is increasing due to rural-urban migration.
options.B: The rate of population growth is increasing due to decline in death rate only
options.C: The rate of population growth is increasing due to increase in birth rate only
options.D: The rate of population growth is increasing due to faster decline in death rate than in birth rate

questions.question: Consider the following statement: "We shall go either for a picnic or for trekking." Which of the following, if true, would falsify this claim?

options.A: We go for a picnic but not for trekking
options.B: Activities such as picnic and trekking are encouraged by the health authorities
options.C: We go for trekking and not for picnic
options.D: We do not go either for picnic or for trekking

questions.question: There were 50 faculty members comprising 30 males and the rest females. No male faculty member knew music, but many of the female faculty members did. The Head of the institution invited six faculty members to a tea party by draw of lots. At the party it was discovered that no members knew music. The conclusion is that:

options.A: the party comprised male faculty members only
options.B: the party comprised only those female faculty members who could not give renderings in music
options.C: the party comprised both male and female faculty members
options.D: nothing can be said about the gender composition of the party

questions.question: There are some nectar-filled flowers on a tree and some bees are hovering on it. If one bee lands on each flower, one bee will be left out. If bees land on each flower, one flower will be left out. The number of flowers and bees respectively are:

Consider the following statements: 1. A man had a wife, two sons and two daughters in his family. 2. The daughters were invited to a feast and the male members of the family went out to take part in a picnic. 3. The man's father did not return from his work. Which of the following statements is true?

options.A: Only the man's wife was left at home.
options.B: It is likely that the man's wife was left at home.
options.D: More than one person was left at home.

questions.question: Geeta: Naresh has become a better boxer since he started meditation. Radha: Impossible. A boxer's most important asset is his aggressiveness. Radha's statement reflects her belief that

options.A: meditation tends to make a person less aggressive.
options.B: meditation has little or no effect on the person who practises it.
options.C: Naresh was a poor boxer earlier because he was not aggressive enough.
options.D: Naresh would not have taken to meditation as he was a boxer.

questions.question: The mangroves can shed tons of leaves per acre every year; fungi and bacteria break down this leaf litter and consume it. they then are consumed by tiny worms and crustaceans, which in turn feed small fish, which feed larger fish and birds and crocodiles. Which among the following is the most logical inference of the above statement?

options.A: Coastal areas cannot have food chains without mangroves.
options.B: Mangroves are an essential component of all marine ecosystems.
options.C: Mangroves have a crucial role in some of the coastal food chains.
options.D: The composition of marine flora and fauna is largely determined by mangroves.

questions.question: "By liberty I mean the eager maintenance of that atmosphere in which men have the opportunity to be their best selves." Which one of the following expresses the view implied in the above statement?

options.A: Liberty is the absence of restraint on human action.
options.B: Liberty is what law permits people to perform.
options.C: Liberty is the ability to do what one desires.
options.D: Liberty is the maintenance of conditions for the growth of human personality.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. Some claim to have seen UFOs (Unidentified Flying Objects). 2. Life on other heavenly bodies is considered to be a possibility. 3. Voyage to space is now an established fact. From the above statements, it may be concluded that

options.B: UFOs are sent from other heavenly bodies
options.C: Some living species in other heavenly bodies are more intelligent than man
options.D: Nothing definite can be said about the UFOs

questions.question: "Price is not the same thing as value. Suppose that on a day the price of everything, viz., coal, bread, postage stamps, a day's labour, the rent of houses, etc. were to double. Prices then would certainly rise, but values of all things except one would not." The writer wants to say that if prices of all things were doubled,

options.A: the values of all things would remain constant.
options.B: the values of the things sold would be doubled.
options.C: the values of the things bought would be halved.
options.D: the value of money only would be halved.

questions.question: "Liberty, therefore, is never real unless the Government can be called to account when it invades rights." Which one of the following is the best justification of the above statement?

options.A: In the realisation that the government can be brought to book in a court of law
options.B: In identifying a man as a political unit in a way which distinguishes him from other citizens
options.C: In a decentralized society wherein the basic needs of men can find satisfaction
options.D: In the understanding that liberty and restraints are complementary

questions.question: The number of deaths among the army personnel is 8 in 1000, but among the civilian population it is 20 per 1000. Which one of the following inferences can be drawn from this statement?

options.A: It is better to join the army.
options.C: Quality of Life Index is very high within the armed forces.
options.D: The groups cannot be compared due to their heterogeneity.

questions.question: Given the statement: "Buses are the cause of more accidents than cars, and trucks cause fewer accidents than buses", which of the following conclusions can we draw?

options.A: There are more buses on the road than trucks.
options.B: Car drivers are more careful than bus drivers
options.C: Truck drivers are more skilled than either car or bus drivers.

questions.question: "If political leadership fails to emerge, there is likelihood of military taking over power in developing countries. Radical student groups or labour may try to raise revolution but they are not likely to compete with the military. Military intervention, rule, and withdrawal from politics is closely related to a society's level of political development." In the context of political development, the assumption in the above passage is that

options.A: political leadership is not an effective instrument.
options.C: military intervention is inevitable for development.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: (i) A primary group is relatively smaller in size. (ii) Intimacy is an essential characteristic of a primary group. (iii) A family may be an example of a primary group. In the light of the above statements, which one of the following is true?

options.C: A group of smaller size is always a primary group.
options.D: Members of a primary group know each other intimately.

questions.question: Geeta is older than her cousin Meena. Meena's brother Bipin is older than Geeta. When Meena and Bipin visit Geeta, they like to play chess. Meena wins the game more often than Geeta. Based on the above information, four conclusions, as given below, have been made. Which one of these logically follows from the information given above?

options.A: While playing chess with Geeta and Meena, Bipin often loses.
options.B: Geeta is the oldest among the three.
options.C: Geeta hates to lose the game.
options.D: Meena is the youngest of the three.

questions.question: Directions for the following 6 (six) items: Given below are six items. Each item describes a situation and is followed by four possible responses. Indicate the response you find most appropriate. Choose only one response for each item. The responses will be evaluated based on the level of appropriateness for the given situation. Please attempt all the items. There is no penalty for wrong answers for these six items. You are the head of your office. There are certain houses reserved for the allotment to the office staff and you have been given the discretion to do so. A set of rules for the allotment of the houses has been laid down by you and has been made public. Your personal secretary, who is very close to you, comes to you and pleads that as his father is seriously ill, he should be given priority in allotment of a house. The office secretariat that examined the request as per the rules turns down the request and recommends the procedure to be followed according to the rules. You do not want to annoy your personal secretary. In such circumstances, what would you do?

options.A: Call him over to your room and personally explain why the allotment cannot be done.
options.B: Allot the house to him to win his loyalty.
options.C: Agree with the office note to show that you are not biased and that you do not indulge in favouritism.
options.D: Keep the file with you and not pass any orders.

questions.question: While travelling in a Delhi-registered commercial taxi from Delhi to an adjacent city (another State), your taxi driver informs you that as he has no permit for running the taxi in that city, he will stop at its Transport Office and pay the prescribed fee of forty for a day. While paying the fee at the counter you find that the transport clerk is taking an extra fifty rupees for which no receipt is being given. You are in a hurry for your meeting. In such circumstances, what would you do?

options.A: Go up to the counter and ask the clerk to give back the money which he has illegally taken.
options.B: Do not interfere at all as this is a matter between the taxi driver and the tax authorities.
options.C: Take note of the incident and subsequently report the matter to the concerned authorities.
options.D: Treat it as a normal affair and simply forget about it.

questions.question: A person lives in a far-off village which is almost two hours by bus. The villager's neighbour is a very powerful landlord who is trying to occupy the poor villager's land by force. You are the District Magistrate and busy in a meeting called by a local Minister. The villager has come all the way, by bus and on foot, to see you and give an application seeking protection from the powerful landlord. The villager keeps on waiting outside the meeting hall for an hour. You come out of the meeting and are rushing to another meeting. The villager follows you to submit his application. What would you do?

options.A: Tell him to wait for another two hours till you come back from your next meeting.
options.B: Tell him that the matter is actually to be dealt by a junior officer and that he should give the application to him.
options.C: Call one of your senior subordinate officers and ask him to solve the villager's problem.
options.D: Quickly take the application from him, ask him a few relevant questions regarding his problem and then proceed to the meeting.

questions.question: There is a shortage of sugar in your District where you are the District Magistrate. The Government has ordered that only a maximum amount of 30 kg sugar is to be released for wedding celebrations. A son of your close friend is getting married and your friend requests you to release at least 50 kg sugar for his'son's wedding. He expresses annoyance when you tell him about the Government's restrictions on this matter. He feels that since you are the District Magistrate you can release any amount. You do not want to spoil your friendship with him. In such circumstances. how would you deal with the situation?

options.A: Release the extra amount of sugar which your friend has requested for.
options.B: Refuse your friend the extra amount and strictly follow the rules.
options.C: Show your friend the copy of the Government instructions and then persuade him to accept the lower amount as prescribed in the rules.
options.D: Advise him to directly apply to the allotting authority and inform him that you do not interfere in this matter.

questions.question: You are in-charge of implementing the Family Planning programme in an area where there is a strong opposition to the present policy. You want to convince the residents of the need for keeping small families. What would be the best way of communicating this message?

options.A: By logically explaining to the residents the need for family planning to improve the health and living standards.
options.B: By encouraging late marriages and proper spacing of children.
options.C: By offering incentives for adopting family planning devices.
options.D: By asking people who have been sterilized or are using contraceptives to directly talk to the residents.

questions.question: You are a teacher in a university and are setting a question paper on a particular subject. One of your colleagues, whose son is preparing for the examination on that subject, comes to you and informs you that it is his son's last chance to pass that examination and whether you could help him by indicating what questions are going to be in the examination. In the past. your colleague had helped you in another matter. Your colleague informs you that his son will suffer from depression if he fails in this examination. In such circumstances, what would you do?

options.A: In view of the help, he had given you, extend your help to him.
options.B: Regret that you cannot be of any help to him.
options.C: Explain to your colleague that this would be violating the trust of the University authorities and you are not in a position to help him.
options.D: Report the conduct of your colleague to the higher authorities.

questions.question: During the summer vacation Ankit went to a summer camp where he took part in hiking, swimming and boating. This summer, he is looking forward to a music camp where he hopes to sing, dance and learn to play the guitar. Based on the above information four conclusions as given below, have been made. Which one of these logically follows from the information given above?

options.A: Ankit's parents want him to play guitar.
options.B: Ankit prefers music to outdoor activities.
options.C: Ankit goes to some type of camp every summer.
options.D: Ankit likes to sing and dance

questions.question: Ten new TV shows started in January-5 sitcoms, 3 drama and 2 news magazines. By April, only seven of the new shows were still on, five of them being sitcoms. Based on the above information, for conclusions, as given below, have been made. Which of these logically follows from the information given above?

options.A: Only one news magazine show is still going on.
options.B: Only one of the drama show is still going on.
options.C: At least one discontinued show was a drama.

questions.question: Read the passage given below and the two statements that follow (given on the basis of the passage): Four men are waiting at Delhi airport for Mumbai flight. Two are doctors and other two are businessman. Two speak Gujarati and two speak Tamil. No two of the same profession speak the same language. Two are Muslims and two are Christians. No two of the same religion are of the same profession, nor do they speak same language. The Tamil speaking doctor is Christian. 1. The Christian-Businessman speaks Gujarati. 2. The Gujarati-speaking doctor is a Muslim. Which of the above statements is/are correct conclusion/conclusions?

Consider the following statement: "Though quite expensive, television is not a luxury item, as one can learn many things through television". Which of the following is the valid inference from the above given statement?

options.A: All expensive things are regarded as luxury.
options.B: All essential things for learning are not luxury.
options.D: Television is not a luxury item.

questions.question: Examine the following statements: 1) I watch TV only if I am bored 2) I am never bored when I have my brother's company. 3) Whenever I go to the theatre, I take my brother along. Which one of the following conclusions is valid in the context of the above statements?

options.A: If I am bored, I watch TV
options.B: If I am bored, I seek my brother's company.
options.C: If I am not with my brother, then I'll watch TV.
options.D: If I am not bored, I do not watch TV.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: The Third World War, if it ever starts will end very quickly with possible end of civilization. It is only the misuse of nuclear power which will trigger it. Based on the above statement which one of the following inferences is correct?

options.A: Nuclear power will be used in Third World War.
options.B: There will be no civilization left after the third world war.
options.C: The growth of nuclear power will destroy civilization in the long run.
options.D: The third world war will not take place.

questions.question: Directions for the following 7(seven) items: Given below are the seven items. Each item describes a situation and is followed by four possible responses. Indicate the response that you find most appropriate. Choose only one response for each item. The responses will be evaluated based on the level of appropriateness for the given situation. Please attempt all the items. There is no penalty for wrong Solutions for these seven items. You have differences of the opinion regarding the final report prepared by your subordinate that is to be submitted urgently. The subordinate is justifying the information given in the report. You would…

options.A: Convince the subordinate that he is wrong
options.B: Tell him to reconsider the results
options.C: Revise the report on your own
options.D: Tell him not to justify the mistake

questions.question: You are competing with your batch-mate for the prestigious award to be decided based on an oral presentation. You have been asked by the committee to finish on time. Your friend however, is allowed more than the stipulated time period.

options.A: Lodge a complaint to chairperson against the discrimination.
options.B: Not listen to any justification from the committee
options.C: Ask for withdrawal of your name

questions.question: You are handling a time bound project. During the project review meeting, you find that project is likely to get delayed due to lack of cooperation of the team members. You would…

options.A: Warn the team members for their non-cooperation
options.B: Look into the reasons for non-cooperation
options.C: Ask for the replacement of the team members
options.D: Ask for the extension of time citing reasons

questions.question: You are a chairperson of a state sports committee. You have received a complaint and later it was found that an athlete in junior age category who has won a medal has crossed the age criteria by 5 days. You would...

options.A: Ask the screening committee for a clarification
options.B: Ask the athlete to return the medal
options.C: Ask the athlete to get an affidavit from the court declaring his/her age.
options.D: Ask the members of the committee for their views

questions.question: You are handling a priority project and have been meeting all the deadlines and planning your leave during the project. Your immediate boss does not grant leave citing the urgency of the project. You would...

options.A: Proceed on leave without waiting for the sanction
options.B: Pretend to be sick and take leave
options.C: Approach higher authority to reconsider the leave application.
options.D: Tell the boss that it is not justified.

questions.question: You are involved in setting up a water supply project in remote area. Full recovery of cost is impossible in any case. The income levels in the area are low and 25% of the population is below poverty line (BPL). When a decision has to be taken on pricing you would....

options.A: Recommended that the supply of water be free of charge in all respects
options.B: Recommended that the users pay a onetime fixed sum for installation of taps and the usage of water be free
options.C: Recommended that a fixed monthly charge be levied on the non-BPL families and for BPL family's water should be free
options.D: Recommended that the users pay a charge based on the consumption of water with differentiated charges for Non-BPL and BPL families.

questions.question: As a citizen you have some work with a government department. The official calls you again and again; and without directly asking you, sends out feelers for a bribe. You want to get your work done. You would...

options.B: Behave as if you have not understood the feelers and persist with your application
options.C: Go to the higher officer for help verbally complaining about feelers.

questions.question: You have been asked to give an explanation for not attending an important official meeting. Your immediate boss who has not informed you about the meeting is now putting pressure on you not to place an allegation against him / her. You would

options.A: send a written reply explaining the fact.
options.B: seek an appointment with the top boss to explain the situation.
options.C: admit your fault to save the situation.
options.D: put the responsibility on the coordinator of the meeting for not informing.

questions.question: A local thug (bad element) has started illegal construction on your vacant plot. He has refused your request to vacate and threatened you of dire consequences in case you do not sell the property at a cheap price to him. You would…

options.A: sell the property at a cheap price to him.
options.B: go to the police for necessary action.
options.C: ask for help from your neighbours.
options.D: negotiate with the goon to get a higher price.

questions.question: You have to accomplish a very important task for your headquarters within the next two days. Suddenly you meet with an accident. Your office insists that you complete the task. You would…

options.A: ask for an extension of deadline.
options.B: inform Headquarters of your inability to finish on time.
options.C: Suggest alternate pe rson to headquarters who may do the needful.

questions.question: You are an officer-in-charge for providing basic medical facilities to the survivors of an earthquake affected area. Despite your best possible effort, people put allegations against you for making money out of the funds given for relief. You would

options.A: let an enquiry be set up to look into the matter.
options.B: ask your senior to appoint some other person in your place.
options.D: stop undertaking any initiative till the matter is resolved.

questions.question: You have been made responsible to hire boats at a short notice to be used for an area under flood. On seeing the price mentioned by the boat owners you found that the lowest price was approximately three times more than the approved rate of the Government. You would

options.A: reject the proposal and call for a fresh price.
options.C: refer the matter to the Government and wait.
options.D: threaten the boat owners about a possible cancellation of the licence.

questions.question: You are the officer-in-charge of a village administering distribution of vaccine in an isolated epidemic hit village, and you are left with only one vaccine. There is a requirement of that vaccine from the Gram Pradhan and also a poor villager. You are being pressurised by the Gram Pradhan to issue the vaccine to him. You would

options.A: initiate the procedure to expedite the next supply without issuing the vaccine to either.
